mission.title: open-webui: feat: whisper integration
mission.description: feat: whisper integration. Requested by 10+ users.

steps.title: Check current open-webui deployment
steps.description:
Verify your open-webui version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n open-webui -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-webui
helm list -n open-webui 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working open-webui installation.

steps.title: Review open-webui configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant open-webui configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n open-webui -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-webui
kubectl get configmap -n open-webui -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=open-webui
```
The current voice input implementation is only supported by Safari. Also, it is highly unreliable due to constant typos and lack of bilingual or multilingual support.

steps.title: Apply the fix for feat: whisper integration
steps.description:
The truth is that there is no simple way to set up HTTPS for a web application that does not require careful attention and expertise of the user. "Easy" HTTPS requires exposure to the Internet in most cases. Running a web application exposed to the internet is a serious responsibility, and it is
```yaml
See "Minimal Private Local LAN Server Example" at https://wiki.nixos.org/wiki/Nginx for more details.

Furthermore NixOS already built-in Ollama as a web service support. The amount of code to enable an Ollama service is:
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n open-webui -l app.kubernetes.io/name=open-webui
kubectl get events -n open-webui --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "feat: whisper integration" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: The truth is that there is no simple way to set up HTTPS for a web application that does not require careful attention and expertise of the user. "Easy" HTTPS requires exposure to the Internet in most cases.
